content:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ausZ_FAATLw

*This is a full guide covering all early player progression, a brief overview of characters as well as a more detailed rundown of Caro aspects! There was a lot, a LOT of content to cover and I didn't have more than a skeleton script so... hopefully you like a meandering voice. I might break this down into a series of smaller videos later, but for now, going to specific timestamps is highly encouraged!*

I know a lot of people prefer consuming or referencing this material in a video or audio format so I gave a try at providing one. Thanks lots for any and all attention given, I hope it helps!

My Caro Beginner Guide might also help, as well as many other resources posted by people: https://docs.google.com/document/d/19oZAR-tf-J668M4lq8wX-SXCiTLlIiqAvXgsAj7gp5g/edit?usp=sharing

thread_title: Beginner Progression Video Guide (with timestamps!) & Caro Realm Rundown

content:
Hey everyone — it’s me, Ernest the stray, still alive.
 Apparently, there’s been a Meta slave boom lately, and a ton of high-potential characters and squads have been buried under the meta overlords of Catena, Corpo, Castor, Tawil and 24, leaving few chances for others to shine.
So this time, set against the Stellar Chapter and D-zone backdrop, I’m here to re-introduce some off-meta Aequor cult teams that deserve your attention.
To borrow a line from a certain Bilibili creator:
> “Everyone in Aequor is a support.”
Aequor characters are absurdly flexible — one unit can fill multiple roles depending on your setup. Swap out a few Wheels of Destiny and Covenants, and you’ve basically built a new team. This flexibility is the beating heart of Aequor: the Versatility Realm.
This guide aims to give Aequor players more freedom in team building — for both main story clearing and D-zone exploration — and to share my current favorite lineup: the infamous Caro-Aequor boy band.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)
